title: Pentagramma pallida
intro: Pentagramma pallida, also known as the pale cliff brake, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds. The pale cliff brake has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain and fighting infections. The bioactive constituents of Pentagramma pallida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the pale cliff brake is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments to support wound healing and skin health.
